steps.title: Identify the time-scale requirement
steps.content: The wet and warm scenario endorsed by Cardenas and Lamb means water was present for a prolonged phase—not brief. Direct evidence would show the basin held water for an extended time.

steps.title: Find evidence of extended persistence
steps.content:
Choice B states the basin held an ocean undergoing gradual sea-level changes over extended time—exactly what prolonged, sustained conditions look like.

A sidesteps the time-scale question, C contradicts the conclusion by pointing to short-lived melting, and D doesn't demonstrate sustained water presence.

This matches choice B — B is correct.
